▶Session summary
Repaired transcript

Your edits, comments, and exclusions — paste this back into Claude to rebuild the report. Untouched items are omitted.

Speaker reconstruction. The acoustic (pyannote) diarization collapsed almost every line into a single cluster, so the 3 speakers were reconstructed from the per-line gender tags (Bogdan is the only man → every (m) line is his) plus role/content to split the two women (Delia = participant, first-person reactions; Ana = PM, facilitation questions). Frames from the recording settled the context: the model is the Transaction Intent Classifier, the two columns are Model prediction / Class, the LLM assist · 20/20 today badge is the “first 20” limit, and the criteria modal is Retrain loop settings.

Phase-1 repairs. Green = applied · amber = confirm · red = needs you.

You

Hand over the session

Drop in the transcript, the recording, and a line on what was tested — all Claude needs to start.

Claude

Repairs the transcript

Phase 1

Fixes the ASR garble, restores the English domain terms, and reconciles who-said-what — anchored by acoustic voice-print diarization, which separates even same-gender speakers. Every fix is logged in the Repair log.

Claude

Extracts the insights

Phase 2you are here

Pulls insight cards — category, quote, confidence — each anchored to its exact transcript lines.

You

Review, edit & classify

Keep or drop cards, tweak the wording, answer the open queries, and sort them by destination database.

Claude

Folds in your changes

Reads your edits, comments, and exclusions, then rebuilds the report with them folded in.

Pushes the final insights to Notion via the Notion MCP — each into the right database.
